About: It is about an Oghuz Turkic, Sunni Muslim dynasty that culturally became Persianate and contributed to Turco-Persian culture.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Khazar chief Tuqaq.
It is associated with the Oghuz Turks.
It is associated with the Great Seljuk Empire.
It is associated with the Sultanate of Rum.
It is associated with the Sultanate of Kermân.
It is associated with the First Crusade.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Kinik branch of the Oghuz Turks.
DevelopedIn: It was developed in West Asia and Central Asia.
It was developed in the Kazakh Steppe of Turkestan.
It was developed in mainland Persia.
It was developed in the province of Khurasan.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Seljuk Beg.
FoundedIn: It was founded in the 8th century.
It was founded in the 10th century.
It was founded in the 11th century.
HasA: It has a descendant of a royal Khazar chief Tuqaq.
It has an Oghuz Turkic, Sunni Muslim identity.
It has a Persianate culture.
It has an empire that stretched from Iran to Anatolia.
It has a clan that split from the bulk of the Oghuz Turks.
It has a leader named Seljuk.
It has grandsons named Tughril and Chaghri.
It has rulers of western Persia.
It has sultans of Hamadan.
It has sultans of Kerman.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being an Oghuz Turkic, Sunni Muslim dynasty.
It is characterized by a Persianate culture.
It is characterized by its contribution to Turco-Persian culture.
It is characterized by its founder, Seljuk Beg.
It is characterized by its descendant of a royal Khazar chief Tuqaq.
It is characterized by establishing the Seljuk Empire.
It is characterized by establishing the Sultanate of Kermân.
It is characterized by establishing the Sultanate of Rum.
It is characterized by being the prime targets of the First Crusade.
It is characterized by originating from the Kinik branch of the Oghuz Turks.
Influenced: It influenced the development of the Turko-Persian tradition.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Muslim cities.
It was influenced by the Ghaznavids.
IsA: It is an Oghuz Turkic, Sunni Muslim dynasty.
It is a Persianate dynasty.
It is a Turkic dynasty.
It is a dynasty that contributed to Turco-Persian culture.
It is a dynasty that established the Seljuk Empire.
It is a dynasty that established the Sultanate of Kermân.
It is a dynasty that established the Sultanate of Rum.
It is a dynasty that was the prime target of the First Crusade.
It is a dynasty that originated from the Kinik branch of the Oghuz Turks.
It is a dynasty that mixed with the local population and adopted Persian culture and language.
KnownAs: It is known as Seljuk Turks.
KnownFor: It is known for being great patrons of Persian culture, art, literature, and language.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Kinik branch of the Oghuz Turks.
NamedAfter: It is named after Seljuk Beg.
OccurredIn: It occurred in West Asia.
It occurred in Central Asia.
It occurred in the Kazakh Steppe of Turkestan.
It occurred in Persia.
It occurred in the province of Khurasan.
It occurred in Isfahan.
It occurred in Kerman.
It occurred in Umman.
It occurred in Anatolia.
It occurred in Syria.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Kazakh Steppe of Turkestan.
It originated in the Kinik branch of the Oghuz Turks.
PartOf: It is part of the Oghuz Yabgu State.
UsedAs: It is used as the official language of the government in Persia.
UsedIn: It is used in the development of the Turko-Persian tradition.
UsedTo: It is used to advise the King of the Khazars.
